programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: Footage of emergency services personnel assaulted in the line of duty. An arrest in Edinburgh escalates, resulting in an officer severing an artery. Meanwhile, PC Ian Bruce, who is part of a specialist team tackling vehicle crime, finds himself alone and vulnerable after members of a gang he is chasing turn on him(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The four remaining contestants face two more culinary challenges, as they compete to secure a place in the semi-finals. First, the four celebrities are split into two teams of two, and each team is tasked with cooking the same dish, which must be identical in both appearance and flavour. Then, the celebrities are then sent out on their first mass catering challenge at London Zoo(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Sharon Osbourne investigates her family history. Recalling her less-than-idyllic childhood and her remote relationship with her mother, Sharon delves into her maternal family history and uncovers the difficult circumstances of her mother's and grandmother's lives. Later, Sharon discovers that she had a great grandmother who was born in the United States, but soon comes to realise that her great-great-grandparent's hope of living the American Dream did not go according to plan when they were faced with the harsh reality of work in a cotton mill(n)

programme.title: Abused by the Police
programme.desc: Following the on-going high profile case of teenager Anna Chambers, who alleges she was raped by two NYPD officers while under arrest in 2017, Ellie Flynn investigates cases of sexual assault and misconduct involving on-duty police officers and women held in their custody in America(n)

programme.title: Supermarket Secrets
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace goes behind the scenes with Britain's biggest food retailers to discover how technology and science are being used to bring consumers their food. In the first episode, Gregg examines the ways that supermarkets are fighting to be first with the latest food fashions, and discovers how online shopping is transforming the way people purchase their food and what they put in their trolleys. He joins Tesco's Head of Plant Innovation as he invents new vegan meals that he claims will taste like meat, and also finds out how Asda are using ingenious growing techniques to make their tomatoes sweeter than strawberries(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles hits the road to catch up with some of the inspirational people and communities the show has helped in its 20-year history. On his travels across the length and breadth of the country, Nick returns to some of the series' biggest and most challenging builds, including Canada Street in Manchester, The Yard in Edinburgh, and DIY SOS's most logistically-difficult and longest build - the construction of the Grenfell Community Centre and Dale Youth Boxing club(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Thousands of people across Britain are being forced into human slavery by ruthless gangs. This Panorama special follows West Midlands Police as they bring down the biggest ever caught. The gang held hundreds of people captive, set them to work, stole their wages, made them eat from skips, wash in canals and threatened to kill them if they tried to escape. The programme hears unique first-hand testimony from slaves who have waived their lifelong right to anonymity so they can tell their stories. It also features exclusive access to the police and legal team who have spent more than four years bringing gang members to justice(n)

programme.desc: Removals men are busy at Phil's house as he and Edith make plans for their wedding. The one thing they are sure about is that Bob will be excluded from the event. Roger tells Edith how he will miss her and then announces that he is in love with Georgie, Bob's daughter. Edith explodes at his lack of judgement, but after swiftly changing the subject, Roger gets her agreement for a wedding video, which he says will be his present to her and Phil. However, he then proceeds to put Georgie behind the camera. Comedy, starring Alison Steadman, Jason Watkins and John Cleese(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef

The celebrity judges deliver some frank and cutting responses to some of the dishes, but others fare much better(n)

programme.title: Scarborough
programme.desc: Derren Litten's comedy following a motley band of Scarborough residents who are bonded by family, friendship and their love of karaoke. It opens a week after a devastated Karen caught Mike drunkenly kissing another woman at the back of a seafront crab stall. It's only when they bump into each other at their regular Friday karaoke night that Mike finally has a chance to explain. But will Karen forgive him? Starring Jason Manford, Catherine Tyldesley and Stephanie Cole(n)

A heavenly messenger is sent to tell him he should have died and that his presence is required in the afterlife, but he remains determined to continue his life on Earth and insists on a trial to resolve the issue. Powell and Pressburger's romantic fantasy, starring David Niven, Kim Hunter, Roger Livesey and Marius Goring(n)

programme.desc: Fact-based Second World War spy adventure about Eddie Chapman, a bank robber who became a British double agent in exchange for money and a full pardon. With Christopher Plummer, Trevor Howard, Yul Brynner, Romy Schneider and Claudine Auger(n)

programme.desc: Sonnaz Nooranvary takes on the repair of a wooden barber's chair that once took pride of place in an Essex shop, while Steve Fletcher and Dominic Chinea restore a clock. Silversmith Brenton West is on hand to fix a precious metal box that is tarnished, tattered and coming apart at the seams. Made of a delicate metal called antimony, it poses a real challenge for Brenton, who isn't helped when Steve warns him of his own youthful experience trying to repair antimony(n)

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: In the Highlands, new entrant crofters Lynn and Sandra welcome more new sheep and take delivery of a batch of hens that they hope will lay the foundations of an 'egg empire'. In Northern Ireland, the Rodgers host a barbecue for the local Young Farmers Club, but it is no ordinary flame-grilled affair, while Raymond and Johnny gather the flock to wean this year's lambs in the Cairngorms(n)

programme.title: China: A New World Order
programme.desc: The second part of the documentary examines how President Xi Jinping has made China a more assertive presence on the world stage since his accession in 2013. For decades, Beijing followed a policy of 'hide your strength and bide your time', but that has changed - creating alarm in the West over China's growing influence. As Xi faces his greatest test yet - a confrontation in Hong Kong - how will the newly resurgent China react?(n)

He tries out Sarajevo's abandoned bobsleigh track, a remnant of the 1984 winter Olympics, stays in a 'war hostel', and meets a man who claims to have discovered the world's largest pyramid(n)

programme.title: Sacred Wonders
programme.desc: A Buddhist painter battles the elements at Boudhanath Stupa in Nepal to honour Buddha's birthday, and in Iraq, at Lalish Temple, a young Yazedi woman goes in search of comfort after being traumatised by the recent conflict. At the Holy Sepulchre in Jerusalem - where a miracle is believed to happen at the same place, at the same time every year - an orthodox Christian delivers a holy flame into the hands of a bishop. Documentary showcasing what people do for faith in some of the most stunning sacred places on Earth.(n)

programme.title: Blitz Cities
programme.desc: Birmingham's factories were crucial to war production, and though the city was heavily bombed during the Second World War, much of the destruction was kept secret. Hollywood actor David Harewood takes an aerial journey over his home city to see the land from the perspective of the German pilots and talks to survivors on the ground about their recollections of the attacks(n)

programme.desc: British PoWs in a German camp try to escape their captors by taking up gymnastics, and using a vaulting horse to conceal the tunnel they are digging. However, even if they manage to escape the camp, the journey to neutral territory will not be an easy one. Fact-based Second World War drama, starring Leo Genn, David Tomlinson, Anthony Steel and David Greene(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: People are invited to try to make money out of their antiques by taking them to auction(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize(n)

programme.title: Born to Be Wild
programme.desc: The SSPCA team receives a delivery of a nest of orphaned red squirrel kits, while head vet Romain Pizzi uses an ancient falconer's trick to get an injured bird flying again after severe wing damage(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The experts take on the challenges of restoring three cherished family heirlooms, bringing back to life a painting, a ceramic plaque and a Japanese lacquered jewellery box(n)

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys grills four more contenders on their chosen subjects, which are The Islands of Great Britain, the ‘Pop Larkin Chronicles' by HE Bates, The League of Gentlemen and Doctor Who, followed by the general knowledge round(n)

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul and Bob fish for grayling on the stunning River Ure in the heart of the Yorkshire Dales, where they talk about their plans for retirement and Bob reveals his hopes and dreams for the future. Together they meet the surgeon who performed Bob's life-saving heart bypass and re-enact all the drama of the operating table - in a local pub. In the evening, Paul organises some entertainment, while Bob struggles to cook a heart healthy dinner to round off their trip(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Adam Frost gives advice on how to prune overgrown evergreen shrubs and adds to his borders to create height. Rachel de Thame shows how to maintain containers and refresh with plants that will extend the display into autumn, and there's a feature on a gardener whose frequent absences from home have influenced the design and planting of his garden(n)

programme.title: Fosse/Verdon
programme.desc: The rehearsals for Chicago are finally under way, having been delayed by Fosse's hospitalisation for a heart attack, but his insecurities soon come to the fore. Bob is hard on Gwen in rehearsals and suggests she is having problems keeping up with the younger cast, while she is concerned about the ending of the show and asks for a new song(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: The long-running comedy quiz returns for another series, with Sandi Toksvig joined by panellists Loyiso Gola, Jason Manford, Sarah Millican and Alan Davies to answer questions about quirky topics. As ever, the panellists will not necessarily be expected to deliver the correct answers, and Sandi will be handing out points to those who provide the 'most interesting' guesses(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Kirsty Wark(n)

programme.title: Front Row Late
programme.desc: Mary Beard presents the cultural review show. As anticipation mounts around the publication of David Cameron's autobiography, a panel discusses the art of the memoir(n)

programme.title: The Levelling (2016)
programme.desc: A trainee vet returns to her family farm after her brother's suicide and attempts to build bridges with her estranged father. Drama, starring Ellie Kendrick, David Troughton and Jack Holden(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Johnny Kingdom's Year with the Birds
programme.desc: As spring makes way for summer in Exmoor, wildlife expert Johnny Kingdom is relieved to discover a healthy brood of wren chicks and finds that some of the adults survived the harsh winter. He is also pleased with his footage of young swallows, and returns to one of his old hides in a bid to film the offspring of a pair of barn owls, as well as enlisting the help of an old friend to record the behaviour of woodpecker chicks(n)

programme.title: A History of Scotland
programme.desc: Neil Oliver explores the contrasting ways in which Mary, Queen of Scots and James VI forged political union with England and established Great Britain. While Mary aimed to seize the English throne from Elizabeth I, her son's method was perhaps even more radical and certainly had the longer-lasting effect of creating the Protestant nation that exists to this day(n)

programme.title: Raiders of the Lost Past with Janina Ramirez
programme.desc: Dr Janina Ramirez looks back at the discovery of three extraordinary pre-war treasures, and the explorer-archaeologists who came across them. In the first episode, she explores the surprise discovery in a Suffolk garden of the Sutton Hoo Hoard, an incredible Anglo-Saxon ship-burial, dating from the early 7th century AD(n)

programme.title: A Timewatch Guide
programme.desc: In the year 793, Europe changed for ever when the famous monastery at Lindisfarne on the Northumbrian coast was suddenly attacked and looted by seafaring Scandinavians. Alice Roberts examines how the story of the Vikings has been portrayed on TV since the 1960s. She investigates how the focus has shifted from viewing them as brutal barbarians to pioneering traders, able to integrate into multiple cultures. She also considers how their huge trading empire spread and their surprising legacy in the modern world(n)

programme.title: The Wonderful World of Blood - with Michael Mosley
programme.desc: Documentary in which Michael Mosley undergoes six experiments in order to take an in-depth look at blood, including injecting it with snake venom to demonstrate its ability to clot within seconds, and starving it of oxygen to see how it instantly responds. Michael also discovers how it tastes when he tries some black pudding made from his own blood(n)

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: Documentary about the history of darts, as it transformed from a traditional working-class pub game into a world-recognised sport, and the role television played in elevating competitors such as Alan Evans and Jocky Wilson into household names. With contributions from Eric Bristow, Bobby George, John Lowe and Phil Taylor. Narrated by Siobhan Finneran(n)

programme.title: British Art at War: Bomberg, Sickert and Nash
programme.desc: Presenter Andrew Graham-Dixon's assessment of three artists' responses to the First World War continues as he focuses on the work of Walter Sickert, an actor and painter who was regarded as too old to fight. Instead, he created edgy, compelling and subtle pictures of those who had also been left behind and were attempting to get on with their lives, despite the conflict and distress around them(n)

programme.title: A History of Scotland
programme.desc: Neil Oliver explores the contrasting ways in which Mary, Queen of Scots and James VI forged political union with England and established Great Britain. While Mary aimed to seize the English throne from Elizabeth I, her son's method was perhaps even more radical and certainly had the longer-lasting effect of creating the Protestant nation that exists to this day(n)

programme.title: Raiders of the Lost Past with Janina Ramirez
programme.desc: Dr Janina Ramirez looks back at the discovery of three extraordinary pre-war treasures, and the explorer-archaeologists who came across them. In the first episode, she explores the surprise discovery in a Suffolk garden of the Sutton Hoo Hoard, an incredible Anglo-Saxon ship-burial, dating from the early 7th century AD(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Peter Powell and Nicky Campbell introduce the edition first aired on June 2, featuring music by Aswad, Desireless, Tiffany, the Timelords, Voice of the Beehive, Five Star, Aztec Camera, Rod Stewart, Wet Wet Wet and Belinda Carlisle(n)

programme.title: Maps: Power, Plunder and Possession
programme.desc: Professor Jerry Brotton explains the creation and importance of maps, discovering the latest technology being used to improve the cartographer's art and revolutionise mankind's knowledge of the world. On a visit to the oldest known map, etched into a hillside 3,000 years ago, he considers how different cultures have approached map-making over millennia, often as a tool for expansionism and political control(n)

programme.title: Lost Films of WW2
programme.desc: Part one of two. Rarely-seen home movies are shown together for the first time to retell the story of Britain at war from a uniquely vivid and personal perspective. Cine-reel footage is interwoven with testimony from members of the filmmakers' families and those who lived through the war, to give a first-hand account of life during the conflict. From footage shot by a Middlesbrough dentist who witnessed the rise of Nazism on a family holiday to the continent in 1939, to images of the evacuation of Dunkirk secretly recorded by a young naval officer, the films show momentous events through the eyes of the ordinary people caught up in them(n)

programme.title: The Most Courageous Raid of WWII
programme.desc: Paddy Ashdown tells the story of the 'Cockleshell Heroes', and their part in defending Britain as it struggled against Nazi Germany. In 1942, the 12-strong unit of Royal Marine commandos used canoes designed by their leader, Major Herbert George 'Blondie' Hasler, to navigate the most heavily defended estuary in Europe, dodging searchlights and machine gun posts to destroy enemy shipping in Bordeaux harbour, 70 miles downriver. The documentary re-creates parts of the raid, and reveals how Major Hasler came to be hailed as one of the greatest heroes of the Second World War(n)

programme.title: Blackadder
programme.desc: Edmund Blackadder, conniving courtier to Elizabeth I, takes in a spirited lad as his manservant - unaware the fellow is actually a woman out to make her fortune. Classic comedy, starring Rowan Atkinson, Miranda Richardson and Stephen Fry, with a guest appearance by Rik Mayall as Edmund's best friend Lord Flashheart(n)

programme.title: Blackadder
programme.desc: Edmund is appointed Lord High Executioner and moves a beheading forward from Wednesday to Monday so he and his staff can enjoy some time off. Unfortunately, he didn't take into account the Queen's tendency to change her mind. Comedy, starring Rowan Atkinson, Tony Robinson and Miranda Richardson(n)

programme.title: Blackadder
programme.desc: Edmund becomes jealous of the adulation that a boastful Sir Walter Raleigh has been receiving in court since his return from the New World. Attempting to impress the queen, he announces his intention to go on a voyage around the Cape of Good Hope, but his choice of captain leaves a lot to be desired. Comedy, guest starring Tom Baker and Simon Jones, with Rowan Atkinson(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Peter Powell and Nicky Campbell introduce the edition first aired on June 2, featuring music by Aswad, Desireless, Tiffany, the Timelords, Voice of the Beehive, Five Star, Aztec Camera, Rod Stewart, Wet Wet Wet and Belinda Carlisle(n)

programme.title: British Art at War: Bomberg, Sickert and Nash
programme.desc: Presenter Andrew Graham-Dixon's assessment of three artists' responses to the First World War concludes as he focuses on the work of David Bomberg. Recognised as one of the most original painters of his generation, Bomberg's early modernist works pushed art to its limits. Born in London's East End, the artist spent his life searching for order in an increasingly disordered world, and his journey took him as far as Palestine before settling in Spain, where he died in 1957(n)

programme.desc: Suzy Klein welcomes the Shanghai Symphony Orchestra to the Royal Albert Hall for their Proms debut. programme.title: Smashing Hits! The 80s Pop Map of Britain and Ireland
programme.desc: Musicians Midge Ure and Kim Appleby visit London and Manchester, the two cities that did battle with each other for musical pre-eminence as '80s music turned towards the new sounds of dance. This is a tale of how studio technology changed music - with British bands putting their own unique spin on dance to produce contrasting northern and southern sounds. Contributors include Denise Pearson from Five Star, Soul II Soul's Jazzie B, Mark Moore of S-Express, Shaun Ryder from the Happy Mondays and Peter Hook of New Order(n)

programme.title: BBC Proms 2019
programme.desc: Cerys Matthews introduces an evening of jazz, gospel and Broadway-style music from big-band giant Duke Ellington. programme.title: Synth Britannia
programme.desc: An examination of the musical backdrop of the 1980s when synthpop was all the rage, having been popularised by bands including Depeche Mode, Ultravox and Soft Cell. The documentary also studies how this genre influenced the future of electronic music and groups such as Pet Shop Boys and New Order. Featuring interviews with Bernard Sumner, Gary Numan and Neil Tennant(n)

programme.title: So Awkward
programme.desc: Jas decides to follow in her dad's footsteps and try out for the junior hockey team. Mr Salford was a legendary hockey goalie in his time at the school. When the try-outs are cancelled due to the coach not being available, Mr Salford steps in and offers his services as coach. Mrs Griggs is horrified - she could do without the chaos he inevitably brings with him - but he won't take no for an answer. Jas and her father are really superstitious and when things start going wrong Jas thinks she's jinxed, and then that she's passed on the jinx to her dad. When she's not picked as goalie for the team Jas is actually relieved - it must mean she's still jinxed and hasn't passed on the jinx to her dad. Eventually Jas does need to play in goal, but when she saves a crucial penalty she and her dad are delighted to realise they are not jinxed after all, just accident-prone! Meanwhile, Martha & Ollie get paranoid that Cassie & Maxwell are usurping them. They've taken over their favourite nook in the(n)

programme.title: So Awkward
programme.desc: Mrs Griggs decides that a 'social skills' class could be beneficial for certain pupils - including Martha, Rufus, Cassie and Maxwell. When Mrs Griggs tells Martha a winning personality as well as academic achievement is vital in order to get into a top university, Martha panics and is determined to succeed in the social skills class and prove she does indeed have a winning personality. Mrs Griggs volunteers a reluctant Mr Malone to take the classes and hands him a book written by her beloved 'Guru Holeman' called Being Whole as a manual to help him. When Lily is mistakenly led to believe by Jas that Matt is emigrating to Australia she is distraught that he hasn't even told her he's leaving. She becomes convinced it's because she's too easy-going that he hasn't even bothered to tell her. She begs Mrs Griggs to let her go to social skills class, too, so she can learn to be less easy-going. Ollie is then sent there for being too pompous and Jas for being too exuberant. programme.title: The Welsh Italians
programme.desc: Merthyr-born Michela Chiappa presents the first of a two-part documentary in which she explores the modern Welsh-Italian community. As she visits cafes and fish-and-chip shops, she finds that while some traditions are the same, others have changed, and while food and family still unite the two cultures, things are less clear-cut when it comes to sport and which nation to support(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Sharon Osbourne investigates her family history. Recalling her less-than-idyllic childhood and her remote relationship with her mother, Sharon delves into her maternal family history and uncovers the difficult circumstances of her mother's and grandmother's lives. Later, Sharon discovers that she had a great grandmother who was born in the United States, but soon comes to realise that her great-great-grandparent's hope of living the American Dream did not go according to plan when they were faced with the harsh reality of work in a cotton mill(n)

programme.title: Abused by the Police
programme.desc: Following the on-going high profile case of teenager Anna Chambers, who alleges she was raped by two NYPD officers while under arrest in 2017, Ellie Flynn investigates cases of sexual assault and misconduct involving on-duty police officers and women held in their custody in America(n)

programme.title: Supermarket Secrets
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace goes behind the scenes with Britain's biggest food retailers to discover how technology and science are being used to bring consumers their food. In the first episode, Gregg examines the ways that supermarkets are fighting to be first with the latest food fashions, and discovers how online shopping is transforming the way people purchase their food and what they put in their trolleys. He joins Tesco's Head of Plant Innovation as he invents new vegan meals that he claims will taste like meat, and also finds out how Asda are using ingenious growing techniques to make their tomatoes sweeter than strawberries(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles hits the road to catch up with some of the inspirational people and communities the show has helped in its 20-year history. On his travels across the length and breadth of the country, Nick returns to some of the series' biggest and most challenging builds, including Canada Street in Manchester, The Yard in Edinburgh, and DIY SOS's most logistically-difficult and longest build - the construction of the Grenfell Community Centre and Dale Youth Boxing club(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Thousands of people across Britain are being forced into human slavery by ruthless gangs. This Panorama special follows West Midlands Police as they bring down the biggest ever caught. The gang held hundreds of people captive, set them to work, stole their wages, made them eat from skips, wash in canals and threatened to kill them if they tried to escape. The programme hears unique first-hand testimony from slaves who have waived their lifelong right to anonymity so they can tell their stories. It also features exclusive access to the police and legal team who have spent more than four years bringing gang members to justice(n)

programme.title: Kate Humble: Off the Beaten Track
programme.desc: In south Wales, Kate and Teg set a mountain alight and experience the heat of a controlled burn with the local fire service in an area with the highest incidence of wild fires in the whole of the UK. Kate gets the creative juices flowing by lending a hand to the creation of an epic land art piece - a 30ft mandala - a stunning geometrically patterned circle made entirely from natural materials. Teg gets a run for her money, when meeting film actors at a rural Film Academy for Animals, and Kate fulfils her childhood dream with a master farrier, learning how to shoe a horse(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: As a gunshot echoes round Albert Square, events inside the Vic go from bad to worse as someone's life hangs in the balance. Bianca tries to talk to Tiffany about everything that happened with Evie(n)

programme.title: Hold the Sunset
programme.desc: Removals men are busy at Phil's house as he and Edith make plans for their wedding. The one thing they are sure about is that Bob will be excluded from the event. Roger tells Edith how he will miss her and then announces that he is in love with Georgie, Bob's daughter. Edith explodes at his lack of judgement, but after swiftly changing the subject, Roger gets her agreement for a wedding video, which he says will be his present to her and Phil. However, he then proceeds to put Georgie behind the camera. Comedy, starring Alison Steadman, Jason Watkins and John Cleese(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: Four celebrities try to book their place in the semi-final, cooking a two-course meal to be judged by former champions Angellica Bell and Phil Vickery, and 2015 finalist Rylan Clark-Neil. It's only when they bump into each other at their regular Friday karaoke night that Mike finally has a chance to explain. But will Karen forgive him? Starring Jason Manford, Catherine Tyldesley and Stephanie Cole(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Thousands of people across Britain are being forced into human slavery by ruthless gangs. This Panorama special follows West Midlands Police as they bring down the biggest ever caught. The gang held hundreds of people captive, set them to work, stole their wages, made them eat from skips, wash in canals and threatened to kill them if they tried to escape. The programme hears unique first-hand testimony from slaves who have waived their lifelong right to anonymity so they can tell their stories. It also features exclusive access to the police and legal team who have spent more than four years bringing gang members to justice(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Sharon Osbourne investigates her family history. Recalling her less-than idyllic childhood and her remote relationship with her mother, Sharon delves into her maternal family history and uncovers the difficult circumstances of her mother's and grandmother's lives. Later, Sharon discovers that her great grandmother was born in the United States, but soon comes to realise that her great-great-grandparent's hope of living the American-Dream did not go according to plan when they were faced with the harsh reality of work in a cotton mill(n)

programme.title: Hold the Sunset
programme.desc: Removals men are busy at Phil's house as he and Edith make plans for their wedding. The one thing they are sure about is that Bob will be excluded from the event. Roger tells Edith how he will miss her and then announces that he is in love with Georgie, Bob's daughter. Edith explodes at his lack of judgement, but after swiftly changing the subject, Roger gets her agreement for a wedding video, which he says will be his present to her and Phil. However, he then proceeds to put Georgie behind the camera. Comedy, starring Alison Steadman, Jason Watkins and John Cleese(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: Four celebrities try to book their place in the semi-final, cooking a two-course meal to be judged by former champions Angellica Bell and Phil Vickery, and 2015 finalist Rylan Clark-Neil. With their last chance to impress, emotions in the kitchen are running high and the pressure proves too much for some contestants. The celebrity judges deliver some frank and cutting responses to some of the dishes, but others fare much better(n)

programme.title: Scarborough
programme.desc: Derren Litten's comedy following a motley band of Scarborough residents who are bonded by family, friendship and their love of karaoke. It opens a week after a devastated Karen caught Mike drunkenly kissing another woman at the back of a seafront crab stall. It's only when they bump into each other at their regular Friday karaoke night that Mike finally has a chance to explain. But will Karen forgive him? Starring Jason Manford, Catherine Tyldesley and Stephanie Cole(n)

programme.title: Room 101
programme.desc: Frank Skinner invites Kaiser Chiefs' frontman Ricky Wilson, and comedians Joe Lycett and Zoe Lyons to express their anger over people and objects that upset them, as the trio vie to banish their top peeves, annoyances, irritation or worst nightmares consigned to oblivion. programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: Footage of emergency services personnel assaulted in the line of duty. An arrest in Edinburgh escalates, resulting in an officer severing an artery. Meanwhile, PC Ian Bruce, who is part of a specialist team tackling vehicle crime, finds himself alone and vulnerable after members of a gang he is chasing turn on him(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The four remaining contestants face two more culinary challenges, as they compete to secure a place in the semi-finals. First, the four celebrities are split into two teams of two, and each team is tasked with cooking the same dish, which must be identical in both appearance and flavour. Then, the celebrities are then sent out on their first mass catering challenge at London Zoo(n)

programme.title: The Hospital: From the Inside
programme.desc: Part two of two. Documentary about the pressures and demands on the NHS in Northern Ireland. A patient requiring heart surgery awaits his operation. Meanwhile, the neurosurgery team operate on a patient diagnosed with a brain tumour(n)

programme.title: On the Air
programme.desc: Gerry Anderson seeks out the truth about sperm sectarianism in the 1970s. Animation series featuring clay characters that use dialogue from the presenter's BBC Radio Ulster programme(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Sharon Osbourne investigates her family history. Recalling her less-than-idyllic childhood and her remote relationship with her mother, Sharon delves into her maternal family history and uncovers the difficult circumstances of her mother's and grandmother's lives. Later, Sharon discovers that she had a great grandmother who was born in the United States, but soon comes to realise that her great-great-grandparent's hope of living the American Dream did not go according to plan when they were faced with the harsh reality of work in a cotton mill(n)

programme.title: Abused by the Police
programme.desc: Following the on-going high profile case of teenager Anna Chambers, who alleges she was raped by two NYPD officers while under arrest in 2017, Ellie Flynn investigates cases of sexual assault and misconduct involving on-duty police officers and women held in their custody in America(n)

programme.title: Supermarket Secrets
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace goes behind the scenes with Britain's biggest food retailers to discover how technology and science are being used to bring consumers their food. In the first episode, Gregg examines the ways that supermarkets are fighting to be first with the latest food fashions, and discovers how online shopping is transforming the way people purchase their food and what they put in their trolleys. He joins Tesco's Head of Plant Innovation as he invents new vegan meals that he claims will taste like meat, and also finds out how Asda are using ingenious growing techniques to make their tomatoes sweeter than strawberries(n)

programme.title: Tonight

programme.title: Best Walks with a View with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: Julia travels to the Cotswolds, near Cheltenham, to uncover some of the finest views in the south of England. Starting in Winchcombe, she soon discovers an ancient burial mound, historic castle and spectacular views from the top of Cleeve Hill - the highest point in Gloucestershire. The presenter then explores Sudeley Castle - home to Katherine Parr, the last of Henry VIII's wives. She soon finds herself atop of Belas Knap, an ancient Neolithic barrow that contained the remains of at least 38 human skeletons(n)

programme.title: Billy Connolly's Great American Trail
programme.desc: The comedian embarks on a journey across the US, following the migratory trail of the Scots through America - starting in New York and finishing in Nashville, Tennessee. programme.title: Best Walks with a View with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: Julia travels to the Cotswolds, near Cheltenham, to uncover some of the finest views in the south of England. The presenter then explores Sudeley Castle - home to Katherine Parr, the last of Henry VIII's wives. She soon finds herself atop of Belas Knap, an ancient Neolithic barrow that contained the remains of at least 38 human skeletons(n)

programme.title: Pirates of the Caribbean: At World's End (2007)
programme.desc: The swashbuckling rogues join forces with old foe Barbossa to take on villainous Lord Beckett of the East India Trading Company, who plans to extinguish piracy forever. When the group return from their rescue mission, they join forces with the nine Pirate Lords to fight for their freedom once and for all. programme.desc: Tracy is frustrated by a rebuttal, Dan gives Will food for thought, and Jacob covers his true feelings(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates another selection of comical mishaps filmed by viewers. This week, a mechanic uses an airbag as a launch-pad. A moped selfie causes a nasty accident that results in a string of profanities, and a peck from a tropical bird make's Grandad's eyes water. Plus, a video detailing the dangers of wearing a monkey as a wig(n)

programme.title: The Ellen DeGeneres Show
programme.desc: Guest host John Cena is joined by acting favourite Leslie Mann, before Jenna Dewan talks about her book Gracefully You: How to Live Your Best Life Every Day. Plus, Melissa Etheridge performs Shaking, from her album The Medicine Show(n)

programme.title: Superstore
programme.desc: Amy turns a boring Christmas Eve at Cloud 9 into a holiday adventure. Meanwhile, Jonah and Garrett's roommate issues boil over(n)

programme.title: Superstore
programme.desc: Dina and Cheyenne snoop through the staff members' social media feeds after a controversial video is posted. Glenn suspects Mateo is guilty of fraud(n)

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Three singletons try to win the affections of Dom, with the buyer of the clothing picked out by the former goth going on a date with him(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Victoria is rattled by a warning, Nicola takes on a mission, and Jacob makes a big decision(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Tracy is frustrated by a rebuttal, Dan gives Will food for thought, and Jacob covers his true feelings(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers, featuring a man falling off a bridge into a canal, a lion jumping inside a car on safari and a student finding an unwelcome surprise in her toilet. Plus, Peter Stringfellow uses a paintball gun to fire Viagra, Harry reveals the real reason Zayn left One Direction and Caitlin Moran falls off a catamaran. Narrated by Harry Hill(n)

programme.title: The Ellen DeGeneres Show
programme.desc: Tom Hanks talks about reprising his role as Woody in Toy Story 4. Plus, Ellen chats to six-year-old astronomy expert Jerry Morrison III(n)

programme.title: Dinner Date
programme.desc: Helen from Essex is given the chance to meet three would-be sweethearts, based only on menus put together by five potential blind dates(n)

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Three singletons try to impress professional footballer Osebi in Manchester, who then goes on a date with the person whose taste in clothes he liked best(n)

programme.title: Take Me Out
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness presents as Croydon bartender Tommy, prison officer Luke from Swansea, Essex-based salesman Bayo, and travel consultant Rixy from Leeds enters the 'love lift'. All four hope to impress 30 single women and win a date - but must first get the female participants to keep their lights on as a sign of approval(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill unveils more digital disasters. Featuring a wallet and key stealing monkey and a semi-skilled scientist, while helmets are definitely required for a collection of bicycle accidents(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates another round of viewer-submitted comedy clips, as captured via smartphone, drone and CCTV. This time, a hoverboard destroys an office, a man falls through a casino ceiling, and an ostrich takes a shine to a young child's mobile phone(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers, featuring a child who is 'squashed' by a wardrobe, a teenager falling off a cliff, and a paraglider feeling sick during a selfie(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill introduces another selection of viewer-submitted mishaps, captured in video form via smartphones, drones and other home-recording equipment. This time, a drone is destroyed by an errant baseball, a seal refuses to join in on a selfie, and a falling tree traps what seems to be a familiar face in a bog(n)

programme.title: Take Me Out
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness presents a special edition of the matchmaking show, celebrating its tenth series and 10 years since the first edition of its parent show, Taken Out, aired in Australia. In a twist on the usual format, Paddy gives 30 men who have previously appeared on the show a chance to take a seat 'behind the lights', while a selection of female celebrities replace the boys in the 'Love Lift'. Among the stars taking part are The Only Way is Essex star Chloe Sims, and Olympic gymnast Claudia Fragapane. The host also catches up with some of the couples who found lasting love after meeting on the show(n)

programme.title: Singletown
programme.desc: It's the end of their first week, and to celebrate the residents of both apartments hit the town for a hedonistic night out - but could this be their last night in Singletown?(n)

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Meg grows tired of being constantly embarrassed by the rest of her family and secretly books them to appear on Diane Simmons' daytime talk show - where they prove so popular that the network offers them their own reality TV series. Animated comedy, with the voice of Mila Kunis(n)

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter's father moves in and immediately begins to take over the entire household, telling the children which TV shows they can watch, giving Lois unwanted advice on parenting and generally ordering everyone around(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Don assures Denise he was not trying to buy her favours, Sally is pleased when Jonathan begins to settle down, and Jim finds Willmore in the bedroom with Liz(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jim moves back into number 11, Willmore tells Liz the problem is her husband, not her, and Angie asks Steve to model a jacket she has made(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Mike gets involved in the troubles of a newcomer and her unruly daughter, only to learn her dark past is about to catch up with her in the form of a ruthless reporter. Meanwhile, Vernon decides there is money to be made from goat's milk. Jason Durr stars(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica visits a New York auction to buy a valuable manuscript and uncovers foul play among her rival bidders. Whodunit, starring Angela Lansbury as a crime writer with a mystery-solving sideline(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: A radio control system is introduced at the depot, but Jack and Stan bring chaos to the airwaves, treating the new technology as nothing more than a lark(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Dense fog leaves Stan and Jack stranded, forcing them to spend a cold, miserable night on the bus, with nothing to eat and miseryguts Blakey for company(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Temperamental tenant Spooner, a professional wrestler, is confined to his room with a broken leg, and makes life hell for Alan and Philip by playing his radio full-blast at all hours - so naturally they challenge Rigsby to confront him. Classic comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Part one of two. Ma can hardly contain her enthusiasm now that Mariette and Charley have announced their intention to marry. Unfortunately, Pop puts all his finances into bidding for a stately home, casting a shadow over her extravagant plans for the wedding. Rural drama, starring David Jason, Pam Ferris and Catherine Zeta-Jones(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth is asked to vet potential suitors vying for the hand of an Australian heiress and discreetly accompanies her on a rail journey aboard the Plymouth Express, only to find himself investigating a jewel theft. Mystery, with David Suchet and Julian Wadham(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nicholson delays his 21st-birthday celebrations to join Bellamy in pursuit of two armed and dangerous criminals, but takes one risk too many. Meanwhile, Vernon finds himself blowing his own trumpet after hiring out a brass band to Lord Ashfordly. Rural police drama, starring Ryan Early and Mark Jordon(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Carol visits Lynn's with a present for Lorraine, Mark refuses to pull his weight on the farm, and Eric wants Michael and Elizabeth to start talking again(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sally talks to Joe about her tax, Liz tells Willmore she wants to carry on at The Queens, and Denise refuses to talk to Hanif when he calls(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jim feels betrayed by Steve and Andy, who move in with Liz at the Queens, while Reg enrages Alf when he lowers his offer for the shop(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby's former CID colleague Jack Colby left the force under a cloud following a sex scandal involving villagers and officers at Causton police station. Now retrained as a probation officer, Jack is working at a halfway house for reformed criminals, so when he is bludgeoned to death the detective sets out to discover why the past would not leave him alone. With Chris Barrie, Edward Petherbridge, Sue Jenkins, Nigel Harrison and Christopher Fairbank(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Mary's past catches up with her when an unwelcome visitor delivers an ultimatum, as secrets and rifts threaten the unity of the family. News about the running of Downton Hospital puts Violet on the warpath, while those below stairs find social changes are putting their futures in jeopardy and Daisy's good intentions at the Mallerton House auction have disastrous consequences. Final series of the costume drama, starring Maggie Smith, Hugh Bonneville and Michelle Dockery(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica uncovers bitter rivalry at an ice-cream company and it all appears to be tied in with the secret ingredient used to fortify the product(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Japp and Poirot investigate the murder of an actress's estranged husband, only to find in the theatrical world, where deception and play-acting are a way of life, nothing is quite what it seems. In time-honoured fashion, it initially looks as though the butler did it - but subsequent inquiries throw up a veritable rogues' gallery of equally likely suspects. Philip Jackson, David Suchet and Hugh Fraser star(n)

programme.title: Unforgotten
programme.desc: Sunny makes further investigations into David Walker's life that suggest he was a troubled man with a number of issues. Cassie has a prickly chat with Marion about her past and the inquiry reveals her close relationship with an IRA activist. As the police investigation encroaches on their life, Sara and Colin open up to their partners with secrets about their past. Whodunit, starring Nicola Walker and Sanjeev Bhaskar, with Lorraine Ashbourne, Mark Bonnar, Douglas Hodge, Wendy Craig and Holly Aird(n)

programme.title: Unforgotten
programme.desc: Marion jeopardises her marriage by refusing to open up about the past to her husband, Colin is blackmailed even further and the secrets of Sara's past push her family to breaking point. A disturbing allegation about David opens up a second line of inquiry, and when Tessa is called in for questioning again it threatens to ruin her relationships with her husband and son. Cold-case crime drama, starring Nicola Walker and Sanjeev Bhaskar, with Rosie Cavaliero, Mark Bonnar, Badria Timini and Lorraine Ashbourne(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin investigate when the body of missing 10-year-old Jodie Gaines is found in a skip. The pair immediately identify two main suspects - a taxi driver who claims to have dropped the girl off at her guitar lesson, and the teacher who swears she never arrived. The case then takes an unexpected turn, leaving Steel and Phillips with the toughest choices of their careers. Dervla Kirwan guest stars, alongside Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Freema Agyeman(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks, Devlin and Chandler investigate after a man is found badly beaten in a park. They discover the victim, Roland Kirk - who lives in a nearby camper van - had many enemies in the community, but nobody is willing to talk. The case then takes a decisive turn when a local man capitulates after being arrested and points the finger at architect Harry Morgan, whose son had recently turned against him and aligned himself with Kirk. Kevin McNally guest stars, with Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Harriet Walter(n)

programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.desc: Stan begins courting Blakey's demure, well-to-do niece, who tries to smarten him up - but it only takes one meeting with his family to make her realise he is a hopeless case. Reg Varney and Stephen Lewis star(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan and Jack persuade Inspector Blake to buy Arthur's old motorbike - but he runs into trouble on the test drive. Comedy, starring Reg Varney and Bob Grant(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Miss Jones reports a prowler to the police and it is not long before an inspector calls to investigate - but, unfortunately for Rigsby, he is not all he seems. George Sewell guest stars in the classic comedy, with Leonard Rossiter and Frances de la Tour(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Part two of two. Pop can barely contain his glee as his beloved Mariette's wedding draws closer, until a summons from the magistrate threatens to turn it into a far from 'perfick' day. Whimsical drama, starring David Jason, Pam Ferris and Catherine Zeta-Jones(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Poirot visits a garden fete where he meets the son of an old friend and his fiancee. After having tea with the couple, the sleuth suspects that they are both in danger and Hastings' new interest in photography provides him with the clue to solve a mystery involving cyanide, failing brakes and mixed-up love lives.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Sergeant Merton is suspended following allegations of theft made by a woman he was dating, but Mike believes he is innocent and sets about clearing his name. Meanwhile, Vernon becomes concerned about Bernie's strange behaviour. Rural police drama, starring Duncan Bell, Jason Durr, Jan Francis and Derek Fowlds(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim makes a fuss of Frank before she leaves for her trip with Neil, and Eric sets up Elizabeth for a meeting with Michael, but she refuses to see him(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim and Neil decide they want to see more of each other, Rachel and Sangeeta's treasure hunt begins, and Archie is not pleased to see Carol back working at the Woolpack(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Liz is pleased to have the twins stay with her, but does not want them to take sides. Percy suggests Emily drops her protest, and Denise is pleased when Hanif takes her out(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Mike realises Doug must have stolen the Jag, Tracy celebrates sitting her last exam, and Emily writes to the Gazette about the wheel clampers(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby becomes an unwelcome guest at baron Ned Fitzroy's wedding to Beth Porteous when the maid of honour is found pinned to the wall with a dagger. Meanwhile, Cully gets cold feet about tying the knot with Simon and is disappointed to learn the honeymoon to New Zealand will have to be cut short. With his own daughter's nuptials to attend, Barnaby races to unearth the family secrets he is sure lie behind the Fitzroy tragedy. Detective drama, starring John Nettles and Laura Howard(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Plans for the wedding are gathering steam, but Lady Mary's meddling lands Carson in trouble with Mrs Hughes. Cora and Violet clash over plans for the hospital takeover, and Thomas's job search is proving disheartening. Mary comes up with a fresh idea that offers Anna new hope, prompting a trip to London, and Lady Edith faces challenges at the magazine although it's a problem closer to home that reaches breaking point. A day out for the family and servants ends in panic and leaves Robert facing a difficult decision(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica is called to testify in a court case concerning a crooked investment firm, but the key witness dies shortly afterwards. Angela Lansbury stars as the sleuth, with Lisa Eichhorn and Larry Linville(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: A mother of two is found murdered in her back garden. Vera and Aiden investigate the case, and delve into the disturbing secrets and ongoing conflicts that lurk beneath the surface of the victim's seemingly peaceful suburban community. The case takes a more complicated turn when the dead woman's estranged family make an unexpected reappearance, and while several of her neighbours may have had cause to carry out a crime of passion, her relatives may have had compelling motives of their own to kill. Guest starring Rita Tushingham(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin are called to investigate when a man is attacked and his kidney is surgically removed. The focus quickly turns to finding the intended recipient of the organ, and the team is horrified to discover that it has been trafficked into the NHS and given to wealthy patient Joanna Woodleigh. When the case is handed over to the CPS, a conflict of interests becomes apparent as Steel and Phillips go up against George Castle in court. Denis Lawson guest stars, with Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Freema Agyeman(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin investigate the death of middle-class teenager Debbie Powell. They discover the victim had been living a secret life as a drugs mule, and was being led astray by local dealer Gerry Craig. The case has an emotional impact on Castle, who demands Steel and Phillips do everything in their power to bring the person responsible to justice. Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Bill Paterson star(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford sleuth investigates a double mystery connected to the local Greek community - the death of a restaurant chef and the disappearance of a baby - and wonders whether an academic event attended by some of the university's leading classical scholars could shed light on the case. Mystery, starring John Thaw and Kevin Whately, with Jan Harvey, Martin Jarvis and James Hazeldine(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: One of the firm's clippies gets evicted leaving Stan feeling guilty as he was late getting her home, so he persuades Mum to take her in as a lodger. However, he soon regrets making the offer after she arrives accompanied by a large dog. Seventies comedy, starring Reg Varney with Patricia Clapton(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan's plans for a romantic day at home are thwarted by Arthur's ramshackle motorbike, which unexpectedly breaks down on him. Comedy, with Reg Varney(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby tries to drum up support for his local election candidate, but finds Alan and Philip have other ideas about who should represent their interests. Leonard Rossiter, Frances de la Tour, Richard Beckinsale and Don Warrington star(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Part one of two. Dispirited by a wet English summer, the Larkins cross the Channel in the hope that the French way of life will have a beneficial effect on the family. David Jason, Pam Ferris and Catherine Zeta-Jones star(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective sets out for Norfolk when a country publican begs him to help solve a murder - but is disappointed when the innkeeper turns out to be a budding author and the killing fictional. However, he soon has food for thought when a real death occurs - committed by a seemingly ghostly hand. Geraldine Alexander and Ian McCulloch guest star, with David Suchet(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The bobbies of Aidensfield try to get to the bottom of a series of burglaries in which the culprit only steals certain valuable objects, including Tricia's postcard collection. Meanwhile, Blaketon attempts to raise support for his local election campaign, with surprising results. Rural police drama, starring Jason Durr, Clare Calbraith and Derek Fowlds(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jack has another go at Mark for not working hard on the farm, Lynn encourages Lorraine to do something with her life, and Elizabeth tells Eric about her meeting with Michael(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Elizabeth suggests to Eric that they start a joint account, Frank sees Kim and Neil together, and Kathy suspects Kim is having an affair(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Reg is convinced he is going to be fired, Ken is horrified when Deirdre tells him Tracy is not returning to school, and Emily is puzzled by Percy's attitude(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Emily's story appears in the Gazette, the Duckworths worry that Alf will ask them to repay him all they owe, and the CID warn Mike that they suspect him of fraud(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Tommy Hicks of the Midsomer Yeomanry finally has his name added to the Midsomer Parva war memorial, though he was shot for cowardice at the Somme in the First World War. It becomes apparent that time has not healed the rift between his descendants and the residents of Hammond Hall, whose patriarch Duggie Hammond pulled the trigger on his comrade all those years ago. A drink-fuelled mock trial of retired colonel Henry Hammond leads to a killing, and Tom Barnaby has to unravel the tangle of bitterness linking property developer Dave Hicks's brood with their neighbours. Drama, guest starring Donald Sinden and George Cole, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Mr Carson and Mrs Hughes' wedding is fast approaching, but a misunderstanding threatens to derail the occasion, and when the big day arrives it also brings a few unexpected guests. Meanwhile, Edith reconnects with an old acquaintance, who comes to her aid in her hour of need, and over at the Dower House, Spratt has something to hide, meaning Denker is hot on his trail. Daisy jumps to conclusions about one of the countess's plots, and tensions mount over the hospital takeover, prompting Isobel to offend a friend. Period drama, starring Jim Carter, Phyllis Logan, Elizabeth McGovern and Laura Carmichael(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica tackles a case in Osaka, Japan, where a motorcyclist romancing the daughter of a traditionally minded family finds his life on the line. Angela Lansbury stars(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway and Lewis struggle to determine whether a death during a college-based clinical trial for a new antidepressant was murder or suicide, and receive little assistance from the institution's evasive psychiatric professor. A second student's demise then leads to the revelation that the trial's participants are all operating under the influence of a mind-altering drug, and that the lines between love, obsession and madness have become dangerously blurred. Guest starring Douglas Henshall (Primeval), Lucy Liemann (Rev) and Thomas Brodie Sangster (Love Actually)(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: Sam and Foyle are shot at and almost run over by a speeding vehicle, and discover the incident is part of an illicit fuel racket. An undercover investigation reveals the detective's son Andrew to be involved, and the discovery that a driver at the petrol company has been murdered complicates matters further. Wartime drama, starring Michael Kitchen, Honeysuckle Weeks and Julian Ovenden(n)

A disturbing allegation about David opens up a second line of inquiry, and when Tessa is called in for questioning again it threatens to ruin her relationships with her husband and son. Cold-case crime drama, starring Nicola Walker and Sanjeev Bhaskar, with Rosie Cavaliero, Mark Bonnar, Badria Timini and Lorraine Ashbourne(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin investigate when the body of missing 10-year-old Jodie Gaines is found in a skip. The pair immediately identify two main suspects - a taxi driver who claims to have dropped the girl off at her guitar lesson, and the teacher who swears she never arrived. The case then takes an unexpected turn, leaving Steel and Phillips with the toughest choices of their careers. Dervla Kirwan guest stars, alongside Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Freema Agyeman(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks, Devlin and Chandler investigate after a man is found badly beaten in a park. They discover the victim, Roland Kirk - who lives in a nearby camper van - had many enemies in the community, but nobody is willing to talk. The case then takes a decisive turn when a local man capitulates after being arrested and points the finger at architect Harry Morgan, whose son had recently turned against him and aligned himself with Kirk. Kevin McNally guest stars, with Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Harriet Walter(n)

programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sally talks to Joe about her tax, Liz tells Willmore she wants to carry on at The Queens, and Denise refuses to talk to Hanif when he calls(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jim feels betrayed by Steve and Andy, who move in with Liz at the Queens, while Reg enrages Alf when he lowers his offer for the shop(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nicholson delays his 21st-birthday celebrations to join Bellamy in pursuit of two armed and dangerous criminals, but takes one risk too many. Meanwhile, Vernon finds himself blowing his own trumpet after hiring out a brass band to Lord Ashfordly. Rural police drama, starring Ryan Early and Mark Jordon(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica uncovers bitter rivalry at an ice-cream company and it all appears to be tied in with the secret ingredient used to fortify the product(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan begins courting Blakey's demure, well-to-do niece, who tries to smarten him up - but it only takes one meeting with his family to make her realise he is a hopeless case. Reg Varney and Stephen Lewis star(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan and Jack persuade Inspector Blake to buy Arthur's old motorbike - but he runs into trouble on the test drive. Comedy, starring Reg Varney and Bob Grant(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Miss Jones reports a prowler to the police and it is not long before an inspector calls to investigate - but, unfortunately for Rigsby, he is not all he seems. George Sewell guest stars in the classic comedy, with Leonard Rossiter and Frances de la Tour(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Part two of two. Pop can barely contain his glee as his beloved Mariette's wedding draws closer, until a summons from the magistrate threatens to turn it into a far from 'perfick' day. Whimsical drama, starring David Jason, Pam Ferris and Catherine Zeta-Jones(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Poirot visits a garden fete where he meets the son of an old friend and his fiancee. After having tea with the couple, the sleuth suspects that they are both in danger and Hastings' new interest in photography provides him with the clue to solve a mystery involving cyanide, failing brakes and mixed-up love lives.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Sergeant Merton is suspended following allegations of theft made by a woman he was dating, but Mike believes he is innocent and sets about clearing his name. Meanwhile, Vernon becomes concerned about Bernie's strange behaviour. Rural police drama, starring Duncan Bell, Jason Durr, Jan Francis and Derek Fowlds(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim makes a fuss of Frank before she leaves for her trip with Neil, and Eric sets up Elizabeth for a meeting with Michael, but she refuses to see him(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim and Neil decide they want to see more of each other, Rachel and Sangeeta's treasure hunt begins, and Archie is not pleased to see Carol back working at the Woolpack(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Liz is pleased to have the twins stay with her, but does not want them to take sides. Percy suggests Emily drops her protest, and Denise is pleased when Hanif takes her out(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Mike realises Doug must have stolen the Jag, Tracy celebrates sitting her last exam, and Emily writes to the Gazette about the wheel clampers(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby becomes an unwelcome guest at baron Ned Fitzroy's wedding to Beth Porteous when the maid of honour is found pinned to the wall with a dagger. Meanwhile, Cully gets cold feet about tying the knot with Simon and is disappointed to learn the honeymoon to New Zealand will have to be cut short. With his own daughter's nuptials to attend, Barnaby races to unearth the family secrets he is sure lie behind the Fitzroy tragedy. Detective drama, starring John Nettles and Laura Howard(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Plans for the wedding are gathering steam, but Lady Mary's meddling lands Carson in trouble with Mrs Hughes. Cora and Violet clash over plans for the hospital takeover, and Thomas's job search is proving disheartening. Mary comes up with a fresh idea that offers Anna new hope, prompting a trip to London, and Lady Edith faces challenges at the magazine although it's a problem closer to home that reaches breaking point. A day out for the family and servants ends in panic and leaves Robert facing a difficult decision(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica is called to testify in a court case concerning a crooked investment firm, but the key witness dies shortly afterwards. Angela Lansbury stars as the sleuth, with Lisa Eichhorn and Larry Linville(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: A mother of two is found murdered in her back garden. Vera and Aiden investigate the case, and delve into the disturbing secrets and ongoing conflicts that lurk beneath the surface of the victim's seemingly peaceful suburban community. The case takes a more complicated turn when the dead woman's estranged family make an unexpected reappearance, and while several of her neighbours may have had cause to carry out a crime of passion, her relatives may have had compelling motives of their own to kill. Guest starring Rita Tushingham(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin are called to investigate when a man is attacked and his kidney is surgically removed. The focus quickly turns to finding the intended recipient of the organ, and the team is horrified to discover that it has been trafficked into the NHS and given to wealthy patient Joanna Woodleigh. When the case is handed over to the CPS, a conflict of interests becomes apparent as Steel and Phillips go up against George Castle in court. Denis Lawson guest stars, with Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Freema Agyeman(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin investigate the death of middle-class teenager Debbie Powell. They discover the victim had been living a secret life as a drugs mule, and was being led astray by local dealer Gerry Craig. The case has an emotional impact on Castle, who demands Steel and Phillips do everything in their power to bring the person responsible to justice. Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Bill Paterson star(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford sleuth investigates a double mystery connected to the local Greek community - the death of a restaurant chef and the disappearance of a baby - and wonders whether an academic event attended by some of the university's leading classical scholars could shed light on the case. Mystery, starring John Thaw and Kevin Whately, with Jan Harvey, Martin Jarvis and James Hazeldine(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan's plans for a romantic day at home are thwarted by Arthur's ramshackle motorbike, which unexpectedly breaks down on him. Comedy, with Reg Varney(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby tries to drum up support for his local election candidate, but finds Alan and Philip have other ideas about who should represent their interests. Leonard Rossiter, Frances de la Tour, Richard Beckinsale and Don Warrington star(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Part one of two. Dispirited by a wet English summer, the Larkins cross the Channel in the hope that the French way of life will have a beneficial effect on the family. David Jason, Pam Ferris and Catherine Zeta-Jones star(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective sets out for Norfolk when a country publican begs him to help solve a murder - but is disappointed when the innkeeper turns out to be a budding author and the killing fictional. However, he soon has food for thought when a real death occurs - committed by a seemingly ghostly hand. Geraldine Alexander and Ian McCulloch guest star, with David Suchet(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The bobbies of Aidensfield try to get to the bottom of a series of burglaries in which the culprit only steals certain valuable objects, including Tricia's postcard collection. Meanwhile, Blaketon attempts to raise support for his local election campaign, with surprising results. Rural police drama, starring Jason Durr, Clare Calbraith and Derek Fowlds(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jack has another go at Mark for not working hard on the farm, Lynn encourages Lorraine to do something with her life, and Elizabeth tells Eric about her meeting with Michael(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Elizabeth suggests to Eric that they start a joint account, Frank sees Kim and Neil together, and Kathy suspects Kim is having an affair(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Reg is convinced he is going to be fired, Ken is horrified when Deirdre tells him Tracy is not returning to school, and Emily is puzzled by Percy's attitude(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Emily's story appears in the Gazette, the Duckworths worry that Alf will ask them to repay him all they owe, and the CID warn Mike that they suspect him of fraud(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Tommy Hicks of the Midsomer Yeomanry finally has his name added to the Midsomer Parva war memorial, though he was shot for cowardice at the Somme in the First World War. It becomes apparent that time has not healed the rift between his descendants and the residents of Hammond Hall, whose patriarch Duggie Hammond pulled the trigger on his comrade all those years ago. A drink-fuelled mock trial of retired colonel Henry Hammond leads to a killing, and Tom Barnaby has to unravel the tangle of bitterness linking property developer Dave Hicks's brood with their neighbours. Drama, guest starring Donald Sinden and George Cole, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Mr Carson and Mrs Hughes' wedding is fast approaching, but a misunderstanding threatens to derail the occasion, and when the big day arrives it also brings a few unexpected guests. Meanwhile, Edith reconnects with an old acquaintance, who comes to her aid in her hour of need, and over at the Dower House, Spratt has something to hide, meaning Denker is hot on his trail. Daisy jumps to conclusions about one of the countess's plots, and tensions mount over the hospital takeover, prompting Isobel to offend a friend. Period drama, starring Jim Carter, Phyllis Logan, Elizabeth McGovern and Laura Carmichael(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica tackles a case in Osaka, Japan, where a motorcyclist romancing the daughter of a traditionally minded family finds his life on the line. Angela Lansbury stars(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway and Lewis struggle to determine whether a death during a college-based clinical trial for a new antidepressant was murder or suicide, and receive little assistance from the institution's evasive psychiatric professor. A second student's demise then leads to the revelation that the trial's participants are all operating under the influence of a mind-altering drug, and that the lines between love, obsession and madness have become dangerously blurred. Guest starring Douglas Henshall (Primeval), Lucy Liemann (Rev) and Thomas Brodie Sangster (Love Actually)(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: Sam and Foyle are shot at and almost run over by a speeding vehicle, and discover the incident is part of an illicit fuel racket. An undercover investigation reveals the detective's son Andrew to be involved, and the discovery that a driver at the petrol company has been murdered complicates matters further. Wartime drama, starring Michael Kitchen, Honeysuckle Weeks and Julian Ovenden(n)

programme.title: British Touring Cars - Crashes and Smashes
programme.desc: A look back at some of the most spectacular crashes in the history of the British Touring Car Championship. Featuring interviews with Tim Harvey, Tom Chilton and Mat Jackson(n)

programme.title: Only When I Laugh
programme.desc: Figgis eavesdrops on a conversation between members of the hospital staff, and promptly passes on the gossip to all and sundry. James Bolam stars(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: Part one of two. Theo makes a deal with a businessman facing prison to go undercover and help bring down a loan shark. Drama, guest starring Eli Wallach and Jerry Orbach(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan gets caught in the crossfire between the family of a recently deceased gangland leader and the members of the late man's crime syndicate, regarding the division of the hoodlum's wealth. The hitherto innocent son manages to play-off his parent's ex-cohorts against one another with increasing dexterity and appears only too keen to pick up the duplicitous traits of his late father. Hywel Bennett guest stars, with John Thaw(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur makes his way in the glamorous world of fashion by selling on some fake designer label clothes to a buyer, only to find himself relegated to minding the frocks. Vintage comedy drama, starring George Cole and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: Cycling: Vuelta a Espana
programme.desc: Action from the 10th stage of the Grand Tour race - a 36.2km individual time-trial from Jurancon to Pau. Gary Imlach presents, with reports from Daniel Friebe and commentary by Ned Boulting and David Millar(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: A ministerial secretary innocently leaks information regarding a secret peace talks meeting to her lover, leading to a shooting incident and placing the future of the summit in jeopardy. While her boyfriend's involvement remains in doubt, Cowley gets her to pass on false information in the hope it will prove his innocence or confirm his guilt. Drama, starring Gordon Jackson, Lewis Collins and Martin Shaw(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Simon Templar investigates a twisted tale of rock 'n' roll, meditation and murder. Anna Carteret and Trevor Bannister guest star, with Roger Moore(n)

programme.desc: Part two of two. Theo makes a deal with a businessman facing prison to go undercover and help bring down a loan shark. Drama, guest starring Eli Wallach and Jerry Orbach(n)

programme.desc: The pathologist investigates the death of a child with a rare disease, and a new colleague of Quincy working in the lab believes the child was murdered by its father(n)

programme.desc: The lure of easy money beckons once again as Terry and Arthur appreciate the commercial possibilities of cheap tobacco, but it is not long before the would-be smugglers are all at sea. Comedy, starring George Cole, Dennis Waterman and Ray Winstone(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: An old adversary of Doyle's stirs up rivalry between factions of political terrorist group the Organization as a diversion in order to launch a revenge attack on the CI5 operative, who he blames for the death of his brother. Action drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan encounters a former nemesis at a party, who drunkenly admits involvement in an earlier robbery in which he was let go due to lack of evidence. programme.title: Killer in Paradise (2018)
programme.desc: While on a dream vacation with her husband, a woman becomes a target after she starts to suspect one of the island's locals may have committed multiple murders. Thriller, starring Jackie Moore, Barbie Castro, Brian Gross, Jordi Vilasuso, Brian Patrick Clarke and Jacy King(n)

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebe catches chickenpox just as her seafaring boyfriend returns home. Joey gets a job at Chandler's company and Monica becomes obsessed with proving Richard is more neurotic than she is. American comedy, guest starring Charlie Sheen(n)

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Chandler becomes enamoured with a woman he meets on the internet, and the subject of children proves a thorny one for Monica and Richard. Rachel relives the ordeal of her failed wedding day while serving as bridesmaid at her former fiance's ceremony. Comedy, starring Jennifer Aniston(n)

programme.title: Neighbours
